codes.title: Validation failed
codes.summary: One or more validation errors occurred.
causes: A required field was omitted from the request body.
A field value did not match its type (e.g. string where GUID expected).
A composite rule across multiple fields was violated.
resolution: Read the `errors` array on the problem+json response; each entry names the failing field and rule.
Re-submit the request with corrected field values.
If the validation rule looks wrong, file an issue against the owning module.

codes.title: Required field missing
codes.summary: A field marked as required was not present or was empty.
causes: The client omitted the property from the JSON body.
The property was sent as `null` or an empty string.
resolution: Send the field with a non-empty value.
Check the OpenAPI schema to confirm which fields are required for the endpoint.

codes.title: Field has an invalid value
codes.summary: A field was present but its value did not pass type or format checks.
causes: Wrong data type (string where number expected, etc.).
Value outside an allowed enum / range.
Malformed GUID, date, or email.
resolution: Inspect the `errors` array — each entry includes the field path and the failed rule.
Normalize the value client-side before sending.

codes.title: Invalid argument
codes.summary: One or more arguments are invalid.
causes: Query-string parameter failed model binding.
A route value did not match its constraint.
resolution: Verify all query-string and route parameters match the API contract.
URL-encode values that contain reserved characters.

groups.title: Resource
groups.description: Errors involving the requested record itself.

codes.title: Resource not found
codes.summary: The requested resource was not found.
causes: The id in the URL does not exist for the current tenant.
The record was soft-deleted (`is_deleted = true`).
The caller is in the wrong site context and the record belongs to a different site.
resolution: Verify the id is correct and current.
Confirm the request includes the right `X-Site-Id` (or equivalent) header.
If you expect the record to still exist, check the audit log for a delete event.

codes.title: Duplicate resource
codes.summary: A resource with the same unique value already exists.
causes: A unique constraint was hit (slug, email, code).
A concurrent request created the same record a moment earlier.
resolution: Read the existing record before creating a new one.
Use idempotency keys for safe retries.
Pick a different unique value (slug, code) and retry.

codes.title: Constraint violation
codes.summary: The request would violate a data integrity constraint.
causes: A foreign key would dangle.
A check constraint was violated.
A delete was attempted against a record still referenced by others.
resolution: Remove or update referencing records first.
Inspect the `detail` field of the response for the exact constraint name.

codes.title: Concurrency conflict
codes.summary: The resource was modified by another request. Please reload and try again.
causes: Optimistic concurrency token (rowversion / updatedAt) did not match server state.
Two clients edited the same record at the same time.
resolution: Re-fetch the resource to get the latest concurrency token.
Re-apply your changes on top of the fresh copy and submit again.

codes.title: Invalid state transition
codes.summary: The requested state change is not allowed from the current state.
causes: The workflow definition does not allow this transition (e.g. `draft → archived` without going through `published`).
The resource is locked by another process.
resolution: Fetch the resource and inspect its current status.
Follow the workflow path defined by the owning module.

groups.title: Authentication & authorization
groups.description: The caller's identity or permissions are wrong.

codes.title: Authentication required
codes.summary: Authentication is required to access this resource.
causes: Missing `Authorization` header.
Session cookie expired or was cleared.
Calling a protected endpoint anonymously.
resolution: Sign in and retry with a valid token / cookie.
If using a service principal, confirm its credentials are still active.

codes.title: Forbidden
codes.summary: You do not have permission to access this resource.
causes: The user is authenticated but lacks the required permission.
The endpoint has `[Authorize]` but no `[RequirePermission]` — strict mode rejects it by default.
Calling a Hub-only endpoint from a client tenant.
resolution: Confirm the user's role grants the required permission.
If the endpoint is missing `[RequirePermission]`, raise an audit ticket — strict mode is on in production.

codes.title: Invalid credentials
codes.summary: The email or password is incorrect.
causes: Wrong email or password.
Account exists on a different site.
Account was migrated and password hash format has changed.
resolution: Confirm the email is correct and registered on this site.
Use the password-reset flow if the password is forgotten.

codes.title: Token expired
codes.summary: Your session has expired. Please sign in again.
causes: Access token lifetime elapsed.
Refresh token revoked.
resolution: Refresh the token via `/api/v1/auth/refresh`.
If refresh fails, sign the user in again.

codes.title: Token invalid
codes.summary: The authentication token is invalid.
causes: Token signature did not validate.
Token was issued for a different audience or site.
Token has been revoked.
resolution: Sign in again to obtain a fresh token.
If you are forwarding a token from another service, verify the audience matches this API.

codes.title: Account locked
codes.summary: This account is locked. Please contact support.
causes: Too many failed sign-in attempts.
Account locked by an administrator.
Suspicious activity triggered an automatic lockout.
resolution: Wait for the lockout window to expire, or contact your administrator to unlock the account.
Hub operators can unlock from `Hub → Users → <user> → Unlock`.

codes.title: MFA required
codes.summary: Two-factor authentication is required to complete sign-in.
causes: The account has MFA enabled and the second factor has not been provided.
The site enforces MFA for all users.
resolution: Submit the MFA challenge response (`/api/v1/auth/mfa/verify`).
If the user lost their authenticator, an admin can reset the MFA enrollment.

codes.title: Site context required
codes.summary: The request must include a valid site context.
causes: Missing `X-Site-Id` header on a multi-tenant endpoint.
Token was issued without a site claim.
resolution: Include the correct site identifier on the request.
Re-issue the token with the desired site selected.

codes.title: Session invalid
codes.summary: The current session is no longer valid.
causes: Session was terminated server-side.
Session belongs to a different device fingerprint than the request.
resolution: Sign in again.
If sessions are unexpectedly being invalidated, check the session policy on the site.

codes.title: Invalid password
codes.summary: The current password is incorrect.
causes: User entered the wrong existing password during a change-password flow.
resolution: Verify the current password.
Use the password-reset flow if it is forgotten.

codes.title: Same password
codes.summary: The new password must differ from the current password.
causes: The change-password request supplied a new password identical to the existing one.
resolution: Choose a new password that is different from the current one and any disallowed-by-history values.

codes.title: Registration disabled
codes.summary: Self-service registration is not enabled for this site.
causes: The site has `allowRegistration = false` in its identity settings.
Registration was disabled by a Hub operator.
resolution: Enable registration in site identity settings, or ask an administrator to create the account manually.

codes.title: Registration failed
codes.summary: Could not complete registration. Please review your details and try again.
causes: Email already in use.
Password did not meet complexity policy.
Required profile fields missing.
resolution: Inspect the `errors` array on the response for the specific reason.
Adjust the registration payload and resubmit.

codes.title: Site context mismatch
codes.summary: The request's site context does not match the resource's site.
causes: Attempted to read or modify a record from a different tenant.
Token's site claim does not match the `X-Site-Id` header.
User assumed a site they are not a member of.
resolution: Confirm the site id on both the token and the request header match the resource's site.
Re-authenticate with the correct site selected.

groups.title: Operation
groups.description: The request shape was valid but the operation could not be performed.

codes.title: Invalid operation
codes.summary: The operation could not be completed in the current state.
causes: Resource is in a state that does not allow this action.
A prerequisite step has not been completed.
resolution: Read the `detail` field for the specific reason.
Complete the missing prerequisite and retry.

groups.title: Service
groups.description: Server-side faults — generally transient or operator-level issues.

codes.title: Service unavailable
codes.summary: The service is temporarily unavailable. Please try again later.
causes: A downstream dependency (database, cache, queue) is unreachable.
The service is starting up or shutting down.
Rate limiting / circuit breaker tripped.
resolution: Retry with exponential backoff.
Check the service status page or operator dashboards.

codes.title: Database error
codes.summary: A database error occurred. Please try again later.
causes: Postgres is unreachable.
Connection pool exhausted.
A query exceeded its statement timeout.
resolution: Retry the request after a short delay.
Operator: check Postgres connectivity and slow-query log.

codes.title: Internal server error
codes.summary: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.
causes: An unhandled exception was raised by a module.
A bug surfaced for an unanticipated input.
resolution: Retry once after a short delay.
Include the `traceId` from the response when filing a support ticket.

groups.title: Cluster
groups.description: Errors from the Hub-side cluster provisioning subsystem.

codes.title: Cluster at capacity
codes.summary: The cluster does not have sufficient capacity to provision this resource.
causes: No worker node has enough CPU / memory / storage to schedule the new pod.
Storage class has hit its provisioned quota.
resolution: Scale the cluster (add a worker, increase Longhorn capacity).
Retry once cluster operators have grown capacity.

codes.title: Payment required
codes.summary: A valid payment method is required to complete this operation.
causes: Subscription expired or was cancelled.
Payment method is missing or declined.
Operation is on a paid plan and the tenant is on a free plan.
resolution: Update the payment method in the billing portal.
Upgrade to a plan that includes this feature.

groups.title: Backup
groups.description: Errors from the Hub backups subsystem.

codes.title: Backup already running
codes.summary: A backup is already running. Please wait for it to finish.
causes: An earlier backup job is still active for this tenant.
A scheduled backup overlapped with a manual one.
resolution: Wait for the active backup to complete and retry.
Check the backups list to see the in-flight job.

codes.title: Backup not found
codes.summary: The requested backup does not exist.
causes: Backup id is wrong.
Backup record was purged by retention policy.
resolution: Refresh the list of backups and pick a current entry.

codes.title: Backup file missing
codes.summary: The backup file could not be located on disk.
causes: The backup record points to a file that has been deleted from storage.
Storage volume was reset or replaced without re-uploading backups.
resolution: Check the storage backend for the file.
If the file is permanently lost, mark the backup record as failed and create a fresh backup.

codes.title: Backup test failed
codes.summary: Backup restoration test encountered an error.
causes: The test restore environment could not be spun up.
The backup file is corrupt.
resolution: Inspect the backup test logs.
Re-run a fresh backup and test it again.

codes.title: Insufficient backup space
codes.summary: There is not enough disk space to complete this backup.
causes: Backup volume is nearly full.
Retention policy is keeping more snapshots than the volume can hold.
resolution: Prune old backups.
Grow the backup volume.

codes.title: Invalid backup status
codes.summary: Only completed backups can be restored.
causes: Attempted to restore a backup that is still running or has failed.
resolution: Wait until the backup reaches `Completed` status, then restore.
If the backup failed, run a fresh backup first.

codes.title: Restore failed
codes.summary: The restore operation did not complete successfully.
causes: Disk error during restore.
Schema drift between the backup and the current database.
resolution: Check server logs for the underlying error.
If schema drift is suspected, restore to a sidecar database and migrate forward.

codes.title: pg_restore error
codes.summary: pg_restore returned an error. Check server logs for details.
causes: Backup file format incompatible with the running pg_restore.
Object ownership or extension mismatch.
resolution: Match pg_restore version to the backup origin.
Drop incompatible objects in the target before restoring.

codes.title: SQL restore error
codes.summary: SQL restore was rolled back due to an error.
causes: A statement in the SQL dump failed (constraint, missing role, etc.).
Target database is not empty.
resolution: Inspect the restore log for the failing statement.
Restore into an empty database and migrate data forward.

codes.title: Database connection error
codes.summary: Could not connect to the database to perform restore.
causes: Database is down or unreachable.
Restore credentials are wrong.
resolution: Verify the database is up and the restore credentials are valid.
Check network policies between the Hub and the target database.
